diagnosis: The level itself is technically fine — a short staircase-style descent/ascent with one manageable gap, completed in under 40 seconds with zero deaths or falls, meaning the platforming challenge was trivial for the player. The low rating isn't about difficulty or fairness; the player explicitly rejects the jump mechanic itself and wants a level with no jumps at all, which this level (like prior rounds) still requires.
lessons: If a player repeats the same core-mechanic complaint across multiple rounds despite layout changes, the issue is the mechanic itself, not the specific level design — further tuning of jump distances or gaps won't move the rating.
